name: tmux-integrated
description: Seamless tmux integration for VS Code terminals — persistent sessions, code-command support, and Copilot compatibility

tmux-integrated.sessionName.description: Override the tmux session name. Defaults to the workspace folder name.

tmux-integrated.shell.description: Shell to use inside tmux panes. Defaults to $SHELL or /bin/bash.

tmux-integrated.cwd.description: Starting directory for new tmux terminals. Supports ${workspaceFolder}. Falls back to terminal.integrated.cwd if unset.

tmux-integrated.autoConnect.description: Automatically connect to existing tmux sessions associated with the workspace when VS Code opens.

tmux-integrated.syncWindowNames.description: Control synchronization between tmux window names and VS Code terminal tab names.

tmux-integrated.syncWindowCreation.description: Automatically create a matching VS Code terminal when a window is created externally in the connected tmux session.

tmux-integrated.closeStrayShellsOnActiva.description: When terminal.integrated.defaultProfile.<os> is set to 'tmux-integrated', automatically dispose any non-tmux shell terminal that the workbench spawned before this extension could register its profile provider. The race is editor-side (see README troubleshooting); the cleanup is purely cosmetic and preserves any tmux-backed tabs.

tmux-integrated.terminalLocation.description: Where to open tmux terminal tabs. 'editor' opens them as editor tabs, 'panel' opens them in the terminal panel.

tmux-integrated.pinTerminals.description: When terminalLocation is 'editor', pin terminal tabs so they are not replaced by new file tabs. Uses VS Code's built-in pinEditor command.

scripts.version: npm run changelog && git add CHANGELOG.md
